reason: Bitcoin is showing strong upward momentum and institutional interest is returning to altcoins, suggesting a strengthening bounce. However, the market is facing significant headwinds from delayed political capital, industry consolidations (incubator closure), and profit-taking by corporate treasuries, indicating potential volatility and a fragile rally susceptible to pullbacks.
headlines: A $100 million crypto campaign fund with a pro-Trump vibe so far failed to show up
Billionaire Alan Howard’s crypto incubator WebN closes down
Bitcoin climbs to $67,500, Circle leads crypto stocks higher, as bounce strengthens
Bitcoin treasury firm GD Culture set to sell BTC holdings to fund share buybacks
XRP jumps 6% as exchange data points to institutional accumulation
